title: Multimodal Large Language Models as Image Classifiers
abstract_en: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLM) classification performance depends critically on evaluation protocol and ground truth quality. Studies comparing MLLMs with supervised and vision-language models report conflicting conclusions, and we show these conflicts stem from protocols that either inflate or underestimate performance. Across the most common evaluation protocols, we identify and fix key issues: model outputs that fall outside the provided class list and are discarded, inflated results from weak multiple-choice distractors, and an open-world setting that underperforms only due to poor output mapping. We additionally quantify the impact of commonly overlooked design choices - batch size, image ordering, and text encoder selection - showing they substantially affect accuracy. Evaluating on ReGT, our multilabel reannotation of 625 ImageNet-1k classes, reveals that MLLMs benefit most from corrected labels (up to +10.8%), substantially narrowing the perceived gap with supervised models. Much of the reported MLLMs underperformance on classification is thus an artifact of noisy ground truth and flawed evaluation protocol rather than genuine model deficiency. Models less reliant on supervised training signals prove most sensitive to annotation quality. Finally, we show that MLLMs can assist human annotators: in a controlled case study, annotators confirmed or integrated MLLMs predictions in approximately 50% of difficult cases, demonstrating their potential for large-scale dataset curation.
summary_cn: 本文指出多模态大语言模型分类性能被现有评估协议和噪声标签严重低估。作者识别并修复了输出丢弃、干扰项弱及映射错误等关键问题，量化了设计选择的影响。基于新标注的 ReGT 数据集，研究发现修正标签可显著提升准确率，缩小与监督模型的差距，并证实模型可辅助人工标注。
one_sentence: 本文揭示了多模态大语言模型分类性能评估中的协议缺陷与标签噪声问题，并通过提出修正后的 ReGT 数据集和标准化评估流程，证明了模型实际性能被严重低估。

title: Omni-Diffusion: Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation with Masked Discrete Diffusion
abstract_en: While recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have made impressive strides, they predominantly employ a conventional autoregressive architecture as their backbone, leaving significant room to explore effective and efficient alternatives in architectural design. Concurrently, recent studies have successfully applied discrete diffusion models to various domains, such as visual understanding and image generation, revealing their considerable potential as a promising backbone for multimodal systems. Drawing inspiration from these pioneering research, we introduce Omni-Diffusion, the first any-to-any multimodal language model built entirely on mask-based discrete diffusion models, which unifies understanding and generation across text, speech, and images. Omni-Diffusion employs a unified mask-based discrete diffusion model to directly capture the joint distribution over discrete multimodal tokens. This approach supports not only bimodal tasks but also more complex scenarios involving multiple modalities. On a diverse set of benchmarks, our method outperforms or performs on par with existing multimodal systems that process two or more modalities, highlighting the significant promise of diffusion models in powering the next generation of multimodal foundation models. Project webpage: https://omni-diffusion.github.io.
summary_cn: 针对现有大模型多依赖自回归架构的局限，本文提出 Omni-Diffusion，首个完全基于掩码离散扩散模型的多模态系统。该模型直接建模多模态离散 token 的联合分布，统一支持文本、语音和图像的理解与生成，在多项基准测试中表现优异，展示了扩散模型作为多模态基座模型的巨大潜力。
one_sentence: 本文提出了 Omni-Diffusion，这是首个完全基于掩码离散扩散模型构建的任意到任意多模态语言模型，统一了文本、语音和图像的理解与生成。

title: BEVLM: Distilling Semantic Knowledge from LLMs into Bird's-Eye View Representations
abstract_en: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into autonomous driving has attracted growing interest for their strong reasoning and semantic understanding abilities, which are essential for handling complex decision-making and long-tail scenarios. However, existing methods typically feed LLMs with tokens from multi-view and multi-frame images independently, leading to redundant computation and limited spatial consistency. This separation in visual processing hinders accurate 3D spatial reasoning and fails to maintain geometric coherence across views. On the other hand, Bird's-Eye View (BEV) representations learned from geometrically annotated tasks (e.g., object detection) provide spatial structure but lack the semantic richness of foundation vision encoders. To bridge this gap, we propose BEVLM, a framework that connects a spatially consistent and semantically distilled BEV representation with LLMs. Through extensive experiments, we show that BEVLM enables LLMs to reason more effectively in cross-view driving scenes, improving accuracy by 46%, by leveraging BEV features as unified inputs. Furthermore, by distilling semantic knowledge from LLMs into BEV representations, BEVLM significantly improves closed-loop end-to-end driving performance by 29% in safety-critical scenarios.
summary_cn: 针对大语言模型在自动驾驶中多视角处理冗余且缺乏空间一致性的问题，本文提出 BEVLM 框架。该方法融合了几何结构精准的鸟瞰图表征与大模型的语义理解能力，通过统一输入显著提升跨视角推理准确率及安全场景下的闭环驾驶性能。
one_sentence: 本文提出了 BEVLM 框架，通过将具有空间一致性和语义蒸馏的鸟瞰图（BEV）表征与大语言模型连接，解决了现有方法在多视角处理中缺乏空间连贯性和语义丰富性的问题。

title: An ode to instantons
abstract_en: We present a formalism for semiclassical time evolution in quantum mechanics, building on a century of work. We identify complex saddle points in real time, real saddle points in complex time, and complex saddle points in complex time that reproduce the known answers in classic problems. For the decay of a metastable state, we find finite time and finite energy analogs of the "bounce" which do not have strict zero or negative modes. The one-loop phase of the wave function and the multiplicity of bounce solutions at late times are discussed. The motivation of this work is to learn how to compute decay rates in quantum field theory in situations with non-trivial time dependence, by first taking a humble step backwards to the fascinating world of quantum mechanics.
summary_cn: 本文构建了量子力学半经典时间演化的新形式体系，通过识别不同时空域中的鞍点复现经典问题结果。研究发现了亚稳态衰变中有限时间与能量的“反弹”类比，并讨论了波函数相位及多解性，旨在为非平凡时间依赖下的量子场论衰变率计算奠定基础。
one_sentence: 本文提出了一种新的半经典时间演化形式体系，通过在实时间、复时间及复空间中识别复鞍点和实鞍点，发现了亚稳态衰变中有限时间和有限能量的“反弹”类比。

title: A class of d-dimensional directed polymers in a Gaussian environment
abstract_en: We introduce and analyze a broad class of continuous directed polymers in $\mathbb{R}^d$ driven by Gaussian environments that are white in time and spatially correlated, under Dalang's condition. Using an Itô-renormalized stochastic-heat-equation representation, we establish structural properties of the partition function, including positivity, stationarity, scaling, homogeneity, and a Chapman--Kolmogorov relation. On finite time intervals, we prove Brownian-type pathwise behavior, namely Hölder continuity and identification of the quadratic variation. We then obtain a sharp measure-theoretic dichotomy: the quenched polymer measure is singular with respect to Wiener measure if and only if $\widehat f(\mathbb{R}^d)=\infty$ (equivalently, the noise is non-trace-class), and it is equivalent otherwise. Finally, in dimension $d\ge 3$, we prove diffusive behavior at large times in the high-temperature regime. This extends the Alberts--Khanin--Quastel framework from the $1+1$ white-noise setting to higher-dimensional Gaussian environments with general spatial covariance.
summary_cn: 本文研究由时空高斯噪声驱动的连续定向聚合物，利用随机热方程表征确立了配分函数的 positivity 等结构性质及布朗路径行为。文章证明了淬火测度与维纳测度的奇异或等价二象性，并在高维高温区证实了扩散行为，显著扩展了现有理论框架。
one_sentence: 本文将 Alberts--Khanin--Quastel 框架从 1+1 维白噪声环境推广至高维具有一般空间协方差的高斯环境，建立了配分函数的结构性质、测度论二象性及高温下的扩散行为。
